  -- With ORDER BY in the window, the default frame is "unbounded preceding to current row"

  ) as "array_agg(driverId) without order"

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to() RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
DECLARE
    v_next_date DATE;
BEGIN
    -- Get the next date for the same team (if exists)
    SELECT MIN(TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE)
    INTO v_next_date
    FROM team_attributes
    WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
      AND TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE > TO_TIMESTAMP(NEW.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE;

    -- Update the previous record for this team (if exists) to set its eff_to to the day before the new record's date
    UPDATE team_attributes
    SET eff_to = TO_TIMESTAMP(NEW.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE - 1
    WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
      AND TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE < TO_TIMESTAMP(NEW.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE
      AND eff_to IS DISTINCT FROM (TO_TIMESTAMP(NEW.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE - 1)
    ORDER BY TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE DESC
    LIMIT 1;

    -- Update the new record's eff_to
    IF v_next_date IS NOT NULL THEN
        UPDATE team_attributes
        SET eff_to = v_next_date - 1
        WHERE id = NEW.id;
    ELSE
        UPDATE team_attributes
        SET eff_to = TO_DATE('5999-12-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHERE id = NEW.id;
    END IF;

    RETURN NULL;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

WITH changes AS (
  SELECT
    entity_id,
    updated_at,
    jsonb_object_agg(column_id, value) AS change
  FROM player_stats_changes
  GROUP BY entity_id, updated_at
),
vals AS (
  SELECT
    entity_id,
    updated_at,
    NULLIF(change->>'height', 'null')::bigint AS height_val,
    NULLIF(change->>'weight', 'null')::bigint AS weight_val
  FROM changes
),
with_rn AS (
  SELECT
    v.*,
    row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY updated_at) AS rn
  FROM vals v
),
ff AS (
  SELECT
    *,
    max(CASE WHEN height_val IS NOT NULL THEN rn END)
      OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY rn
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS last_h_rn,
    max(CASE WHEN weight_val IS NOT NULL THEN rn END)
      OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY rn
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS last_w_rn
  FROM with_rn
)
SELECT
  entity_id,
  updated_at,
  first_value(height_val) OVER (
    PARTITION BY entity_id, last_h_rn
    ORDER BY rn
    ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
  ) AS height,
  first_value(weight_val) OVER (
    PARTITION BY entity_id, last_w_rn
    ORDER BY rn
    ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
  ) AS weight
FROM ff
ORDER BY entity_id, updated_at;

WITH RECURSIVE leaf_to_root AS (
    -- start from leaves (nodes with no children); they already have a color
    SELECT s.id, s.parent_id, s.color AS leaf_color
    FROM superhero2 s
    WHERE s.color IS NOT NULL
      AND NOT EXISTS (
          SELECT 1
          FROM superhero2 ch
          WHERE ch.parent_id = s.id
      )
  UNION ALL
    -- move one step up carrying each leaf's color to all its ancestors
    SELECT p.id, p.parent_id, l.leaf_color
    FROM leaf_to_root l
    JOIN superhero2 p
      ON p.id = l.parent_id
),
colored AS (
    -- for each node, if all descendant leaves have the same color, use it; otherwise gray
    SELECT id,
           CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT leaf_color) = 1
                THEN MIN(leaf_color)
                ELSE 'gray'
           END AS color
    FROM leaf_to_root
    GROUP BY id
)
UPDATE superhero2 s
SET color = c.color
FROM colored c
WHERE s.id = c.id;

-- Distinct values for ALL keys per customer (builds a JSON object: key -> distinct values)
SELECT customerid,
       jsonb_object_agg(key, vals) AS aggregated_preferences
FROM (
  SELECT customerid, key, jsonb_agg(DISTINCT el) AS vals
  FROM (
    SELECT cp.customerid,
           je.key,
           a.el
    FROM card_preference AS cp
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL jsonb_each(cp.preference) AS je(key, val)
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL jsonb_array_elements(
      CASE
        WHEN jsonb_typeof(je.val) = 'array' THEN je.val
        ELSE jsonb_build_array(je.val)
      END
    ) AS a(el)
    WHERE cp.customerid = '123.abc'  -- remove this line to compute for all customers
  ) AS s
  GROUP BY customerid, key
) AS g
GROUP BY customerid;

WITH RECURSIVE
-- Retail price per item (some items have multiple rows; take a single price)
prices AS (
  SELECT item_id, MAX(item_price) AS retail_price
  FROM store
  GROUP BY item_id
),
-- Cost from resources only (partial craft price)
resource_cost AS (
  SELECT item_id,
         SUM(COALESCE(resource_price, 0) * COALESCE(quantity, 1)) AS partial_craft_price
  FROM store
  WHERE resource_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY item_id
),
-- Components (other items) needed to craft each item
components AS (
  SELECT item_id,
         uses_item_id AS component_id,
         SUM(COALESCE(quantity, 1)) AS qty
  FROM store
  WHERE uses_item_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY item_id, uses_item_id
),
-- All items we care about with their retail and partial craft prices
all_items AS (
  SELECT p.item_id,
         p.retail_price,
         COALESCE(rc.partial_craft_price, 0) AS partial_craft_price
  FROM prices p
  LEFT JOIN resource_cost rc USING (item_id)
),
-- Iteratively resolve the minimal cost to obtain each item:
-- min_cost = LEAST(retail_price, full_craft_price)
-- full_craft_price = resource cost + sum(qty * min_cost(child))
resolved AS (
  -- Seed: items without component dependencies (only resources)
  SELECT ai.item_id,
         ai.retail_price,
         ai.partial_craft_price AS full_craft_price,
         LEAST(ai.retail_price, ai.partial_craft_price) AS min_cost
  FROM all_items ai
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM components c WHERE c.item_id = ai.item_id)

  UNION ALL

  -- Step: items whose components are all already resolved
  SELECT ai.item_id,
         ai.retail_price,
         ai.partial_craft_price
         + (
             SELECT SUM(c.qty * r.min_cost)
             FROM components c
             JOIN resolved r ON r.item_id = c.component_id
             WHERE c.item_id = ai.item_id
           ) AS full_craft_price,
         LEAST(
           ai.retail_price,
           ai.partial_craft_price
           + (
               SELECT SUM(c.qty * r.min_cost)
               FROM components c
               JOIN resolved r ON r.item_id = c.component_id
               WHERE c.item_id = ai.item_id
             )
         ) AS min_cost
  FROM all_items ai
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
          SELECT 1
          FROM components c
          WHERE c.item_id = ai.item_id
            AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM resolved r WHERE r.item_id = c.component_id)
        )
    AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM resolved r0 WHERE r0.item_id = ai.item_id)
)
SELECT ai.item_id,
       ai.retail_price AS retail_price,
       ai.partial_craft_price AS partial_craft_price,
       r.full_craft_price AS full_craft_price
FROM all_items ai
LEFT JOIN resolved r ON r.item_id = ai.item_id
ORDER BY ai.item_id;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION f_gap_fill_update(tbl regclass, id text, row_num text)
RETURNS void
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $func$
DECLARE
    v_schema text;
    v_table  text;
    fqtn     text;
    col      text;
BEGIN
    -- Resolve schema and table names from regclass
    SELECT n.nspname, c.relname
      INTO v_schema, v_table
      FROM pg_class c
      JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
     WHERE c.oid = tbl;

    fqtn := format('%I.%I', v_schema, v_table);

    -- Loop over all nullable columns except the partition and order columns
    FOR col IN
        SELECT c.column_name
          FROM information_schema.columns c
         WHERE c.table_schema = v_schema
           AND c.table_name  = v_table
           AND c.is_nullable = 'YES'
           AND c.column_name <> id
           AND c.column_name <> row_num
    LOOP
        -- Forward-fill each nullable column within partition id ordered by row_num
        EXECUTE format($sql$
            WITH src AS (
                SELECT
                    ctid,
                    %1$I AS val,
                    %2$I AS id_col,
                    sum(CASE WHEN %1$I IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
                        OVER (PARTITION BY %2$I ORDER BY %3$I, ctid) AS grp
                FROM %4$s
            ),
            fill AS (
                SELECT
                    ctid,
                    max(val) OVER (PARTITION BY id_col, grp) AS filled
                FROM src
            )
            UPDATE %4$s t
               SET %1$I = f.filled
              FROM fill f
             WHERE t.ctid = f.ctid
               AND t.%1$I IS NULL
               AND f.filled IS NOT NULL;
        $sql$, col, id, row_num, fqtn);
    END LOOP;
END
$func$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.generate_series_monthly(a date, b date) RETURNS SETOF date LANGUAGE SQL IMMUTABLE PARALLEL SAFE ROWS 10 AS $function$ select generate_series(date_trunc('month', a), date_trunc('month', b), '1 month') $function$;EXPLAIN SELECT generate_series_monthly('2024-01-01', '2024-05-01');EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM generate_series_monthly('2024-01-01', '2024-05-01')

-- 1) Ensure a unique key exists on (id, stage) so it can be referenced by FKs
ALTER TABLE "match"
ADD CONSTRAINT match_id_stage_unique UNIQUE (id, stage);

-- 2) Replace the problematic FK constraints with cascading composite FKs
ALTER TABLE "dependency"
DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS dependency_match1_stage1_fkey,
DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS dependency_match2_stage2_fkey;

ALTER TABLE "dependency"
ADD CONSTRAINT dependency_match1_stage1_fkey
  FOREIGN KEY (match1, stage1)
  REFERENCES "match" (id, stage)
  ON UPDATE CASCADE
  ON DELETE CASCADE,
ADD CONSTRAINT dependency_match2_stage2_fkey
  FOREIGN KEY (match2, stage2)
  REFERENCES "match" (id, stage)
  ON UPDATE CASCADE
  ON DELETE CASCADE;

-- 3) Now you can safely bump the stage and dependencies will update automatically
UPDATE "match"
SET stage = stage + 1
WHERE id = 'match1';

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION reindex_superhero_ids(IN BIGINT) RETURNS VOID LANGUAGE SQL AS $$ CREATE TEMPORARY SEQUENCE id_seq_temp MINVALUE 1 START WITH 1 INCREMENT BY 1; ALTER SEQUENCE id_seq_temp RESTART; UPDATE superhero SET hair_colour_id=hair_colour_id+2000 WHERE publisher_id=$1; UPDATE superhero SET hair_colour_id=nextval('id_seq_temp') WHERE publisher_id=$1; $$

-- Solution A (recommended if you can change the schema):
-- Make the primary key DEFERRABLE so uniqueness is checked at commit, not per-row update.
-- One-time schema change:
ALTER TABLE card_versions
  DROP CONSTRAINT card_versions_pkey,
  ADD CONSTRAINT card_versions_pkey
    PRIMARY KEY (seqnr, defn_id, attr_id)
    DEFERRABLE INITIALLY IMMEDIATE;

-- Then perform the update with the constraint deferred:
BEGIN;
SET CONSTRAINTS card_versions_pkey DEFERRED;

UPDATE card_versions
SET seqnr = seqnr + 1
WHERE defn_id = 100
  AND attr_id = 100
  AND seqnr >= 1;

COMMIT;


-- Solution B (no schema change): two-step "park then shift" update to avoid collisions.
-- This moves the target rows out of the way, then into their final values.
BEGIN;

WITH oldmax AS (
  SELECT MAX(seqnr)::int AS m
  FROM card_versions
  WHERE defn_id = 100 AND attr_id = 100
),
park AS (
  UPDATE card_versions cv
  SET seqnr = cv.seqnr + om.m
  FROM oldmax om
  WHERE cv.defn_id = 100
    AND cv.attr_id = 100
    AND cv.seqnr >= 1
  RETURNING cv.seqnr
)
UPDATE card_versions cv
SET seqnr = cv.seqnr - (om.m - 1)
FROM oldmax om
WHERE cv.defn_id = 100
  AND cv.attr_id = 100
  AND cv.seqnr > om.m;

COMMIT;
